content: Define the synthesizable SystemVerilog module named TopModule with the exact required port names, directions, and widths. Do not add, remove, or rename any ports. Keep this as the only module in the file.
source:
Implement a module named TopModule with the following interface:

- input  logic        clk
- input  logic        reset
- input  logic        in_valid
- input  logic [15:0] in_a
- input  logic [15:0] in_b
- input  logic [1:0]  in_mode
- output logic        out_valid
- output logic [31:0] out_y
- output logic [7:0]  out_tag

[Strict Interface Contract]
Your generated TopModule must keep the exact port names and widths below.
Do not rename ports. Do not add/remove ports. Do not change bit-widths.
- clk: 1-bit
- in_a: [15:0]
- in_b: [15:0]
- in_mode: [1:0]
- in_valid: 1-bit
- out_tag: [7:0]
- out_valid: 1-bit
- out_y: [31:0]
- reset: 1-bit

content: Create one internal 32-bit combinational result signal for the arithmetic result that will later feed out_y. Implement a single mode-select combinational calculation to minimize duplicated logic: mode 00 performs zero-extended addition, mode 01 performs zero-extended multiplication, mode 10 performs zero-extended XOR, and mode 11 performs the specified weighted sum using zero-extended operands and shifts. Preserve exact 32-bit arithmetic behavior.

Implement one internal 8-bit sequential tag counter signal. On reset, clear it to 0. On each positive clock edge with in_valid high, increment it by 1 to allocate the next issue tag. When in_valid is low, hold its value. This counter represents the next tag to assign and should naturally wrap at 8 bits.
source:
out_tag is an issue tag that starts from 0 after reset and increments by 1 for every accepted input (in_valid=1).

When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out_y/out_tag must match that input.

content: Implement out_valid as a 1-cycle registered output. On reset, drive out_valid to 0. Otherwise, on each positive clock edge, register the current in_valid value so that an input valid at cycle N produces out_valid at cycle N+1, and an invalid input at cycle N produces out_valid low at cycle N+1.
source:
Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out_y/out_tag must match that input.
When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.

content: Implement out_y as a 32-bit pipeline register driven by the internal combinational result signal. On reset, clear out_y to 0. Otherwise, register the combinational result on each positive clock edge so the arithmetic result corresponding to an input at cycle N is available at out_y at cycle N+1. The correctness requirement applies when the associated out_valid is high.

Implement out_tag as an 8-bit pipeline register. On reset, clear out_tag to 0. Otherwise, register the current tag counter value on each positive clock edge. Because the tag counter increments only for accepted inputs, the value captured into out_tag on the cycle after in_valid is high is the correct issue tag for that accepted input.
source:
Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out_y/out_tag must match that input.
out_tag is an issue tag that starts from 0 after reset and increments by 1 for every accepted input (in_valid=1).
